citations.description: search_files for 'homebrew-rebar' returned only two hits in docs/releasing.md (lines 12 and 318) — both pre-existing references to the tap repo URL, not a new workflow file.
search_files for 'brew-bump', 'self-bump', and 'PyPI for the latest nava-rebar' all returned no matches.

findings.criterion: Add a scheduled + workflow_dispatch self-bump workflow IN the tap repo (navapbc/homebrew-rebar) that polls PyPI for the latest nava-rebar, updates Formula/rebar.rb url+sha256 and commits with the tap's own GITHUB_TOKEN; idempotent no-op when current
findings.detail: This workflow must live in the external repo navapbc/homebrew-rebar. That repo is not part of this codebase and no evidence of a self-bump workflow file was found anywhere in this repository. There is no documentation update, script, or CI job in this repo that references the creation of such a workflow. The ticket is untagged (not [operator-attested]) so the codebase-verifiable bar applies, but no code in scope satisfies it.
findings.remediation: Create the workflow file (e.g. .github/workflows/bump-rebar.yml) in the navapbc/homebrew-rebar tap repo with schedule + workflow_dispatch triggers, PyPI polling logic, Formula/rebar.rb url+sha256 update, and a GITHUB_TOKEN commit. Record the PR/commit URL in a ticket comment as evidence of delivery.

citations.description: Line 49 still reads '(still manual — step 3)' — not updated.
Step 3 still describes the fully manual curl + edit + commit process with no automation notice or fallback framing.

findings.criterion: Update docs/releasing.md step 3 to mark Homebrew automated (manual = fallback)
findings.detail: Step 3 of docs/releasing.md (lines 310–326) still describes the fully manual process with no automation notice. Line 49 still reads '(still manual — step 3)'. No language marking Homebrew as 'automated' or describing the self-bump workflow as the primary path with manual as fallback was added.

citations.description: Most recent release is [0.9.0] - 2026-07-20; no [0.9.1] entry exists.

findings.criterion: Release v0.9.1 (version bump carrying the doc change + issue 858 dedup filter)
findings.detail: pyproject.toml version is still '0.9.0' and server.json version is also '0.9.0'. CHANGELOG.md has no 0.9.1 entry — the most recent release entry is '[0.9.0] - 2026-07-20'. The version has not been bumped to 0.9.1.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
